title: I am setting up an Obsidian vault as a local AI me

promptContent.text: I am setting up an Obsidian vault as a local AI memory layer. Generate a folder structure separating 'Church and State'. I need a 'Human' folder for my personal notes, and an 'AI' folder for agent skills, workflows, templates, and SOPs. Output the structure clearly so I can build it.

messages.content:
<smtcmp_block language="markdown">
# Vault Structure: Church & State

- 👤 **Human** (Personal Context)
    - 📓 Personal Notes
    - 🚀 Projects
    - 📅 Journal
    - 🗄️ Archive
- 🤖 **AI** (Agentic Layer)
    - 🧠 Skills
    - ⚙️ Workflows
    - 📋 Templates
    - 📜 SOPs
    - ⌨️ Prompt Library
</smtcmp_block>

I cannot directly access your local file system. To automate the creation of the structure provided, run one of the following in your terminal:

### Option 1: Bash (macOS, Linux, or Git Bash)
<smtcmp_block language="bash">
mkdir -p "Human/Personal Notes" "Human/Projects" "Human/Journal" "Human/Archive" "AI/Skills" "AI/Workflows" "AI/Templates" "AI/SOPs" "AI/Prompt Library"
</smtcmp_block>

### Option 2: Python (Cross-platform)
<smtcmp_block language="python">
import os

folders = [
    "Human/Personal Notes", "Human/Projects", "Human/Journal", "Human/Archive",
    "AI/Skills", "AI/Workflows", "AI/Templates", "AI/SOPs", "AI/Prompt Library"
]

for folder in folders:
    os.makedirs(folder, exist_ok=True)
</
